summary.intent: Add a Nostr DM channel to the Moltis multi-channel messaging platform, reaching parity with existing channels (Telegram, Discord, Slack, MS Teams, WhatsApp, Matrix) including backend crate, web UI, onboarding, docs, metrics, NIP-44 support, and integration tests.
summary.outcome: Full Nostr channel implementation merged via PR #674: new `moltis-nostr` crate (~1700 LOC), web UI add/edit/onboarding flows, 23 unit tests + 5 integration tests with real relay round-trip, metrics instrumentation, NIP-44 fallback decryption, documentation, and website icon.

repo: ChannelType enum is `#[non_exhaustive]` so new variants don't break wildcard match arms in gateway/chat crates
Channel plugins are gated by feature flags chained: cli → httpd → gateway → crate; all enabled by default
Config `ChannelsConfig` uses `#[serde(flatten)]` extra map for channels not yet promoted to named fields; named fields take priority over flatten during deserialization
The `moltis_channels::contract` test suite uses a `TestPlugin` stub, not real channel plugins that require credentials — real plugins need per-channel unit tests instead
The web UI channel forms use Preact/HTM with no build step; `biome check --write` is the linter/formatter
Integration test `notifications()` broadcast receiver must be obtained BEFORE `connect()` to avoid missing early relay messages
nostr-sdk v0.44: `nip04` and `nip44` features are not in `default` and must be explicitly enabled; `Filter::pubkey()` filters on `#p` tag (recipient), not event author
nostr-sdk prelude does `use nip44::{self, *}` so both `nip44` module and its functions are in scope simultaneously, causing qualifier lint confusion
Website locale files (10 languages) were already fully updated with Nostr references — only the SVG icon was missing

The worktree (`/superset/worktrees/`) uses an orphan/squashed history for `main` with only 1 commit, making `git merge main` produce 70+ unrelated-history conflicts; cherry-pick onto origin/main is the correct strategy

code.finding: String truncation at MAX_MESSAGE_BYTES must use `str::floor_char_boundary()` to avoid panic at non-UTF-8 char boundaries
client.notifications() returns a broadcast::Receiver that must be bound to a variable before use in an async loop to avoid temporary drop
OtpState::new() requires a cooldown_secs argument, not a zero-arg constructor
Config must be Arc<RwLock<NostrAccountConfig>> shared between AccountState and the bus loop to avoid stale clones on config update
Allowlist pubkeys should be pre-parsed into Vec<PublicKey> in AccountState and refreshed on config update, not re-parsed on every inbound DM
ALL constant and descriptor() match must both be updated when adding a new ChannelType variant; a test checks ALL.len() == enum discriminant count

workflow: nostr-sdk optional features (`nip04`, `nip44`) must be explicitly listed in workspace Cargo.toml dependency features array
Use `dep?/feature` syntax in Cargo feature deps when the dependency itself is optional, to avoid unintentionally enabling it
When a worktree has unrelated history to origin/main, use cherry-pick strategy: identify commit range with `git log --oneline`, create branch from origin/main, cherry-pick the range
Biome lint must be run on JS files after every edit; run from the web/ui directory
Integration tests that use relay pub/sub must obtain the notification receiver before connecting and subscribing to avoid race conditions

friction: nostr-sdk NIP-04/NIP-44 features not in default required discovery via cargo metadata inspection
nostr prelude glob `use nip44::{self, *}` causes spurious 'unnecessary qualification' clippy lint for `nip44::decrypt` — required using fully-qualified `nostr_sdk::nips::nip04::decrypt` style path
DM round-trip integration test failed initially because notifications() receiver was created after connect(), missing early relay messages
Merging origin/main into the worktree branch caused 70+ conflicts due to unrelated histories (squashed main vs full history branch); required abort and cherry-pick strategy
Matrix was accidentally promoted from extra/flatten map to named field in ChannelsConfig as a side effect; required a separate revert commit
client.disconnect() does not return a Result in nostr-sdk v0.44 but code was written assuming it did
open_items: NIP-17 gift-wrapped DMs: more complex event handling, less widely supported — deferred to future PR
Metrics feature: counters/histograms wired but no Grafana dashboard or alerting thresholds configured yet
Integration tests require NOSTR_TEST_BOT_KEY and NOSTR_TEST_SENDER_KEY GitHub secrets to be set for CI to run them non-ignored
CI workflow file not yet updated to run `cargo test -p moltis-nostr --test nostr_integration -- --ignored` with the new secrets
Outbound NIP-44 preference config option exists in design but NIP-04 is hardcoded for outbound; NIP-44 only used for inbound fallback
